2603.20293.title: LLM-Enhanced Energy Contrastive Learning for Out-of-Distribution Detection in Text-Attributed Graphs
2603.20293.paper_summary: Text-attributed graphs, where nodes are enriched with textual attributes, have become a powerful tool for modeling real-world networks such as citation, social, and transaction networks. However, existing methods for learning from these graphs often assume that the distributions of training and testing data are consistent. This assumption leads to significant performance degradation when faced with out-of-distribution (OOD) data. In this paper, we address the challenge of node-level OOD detection in text-attributed graphs, with the goal of maintaining accurate node classification while simultaneously identifying OOD nodes. We propose a novel approach, LLM-Enhanced Energy Contrastive Learning for Out-of-Distribution Detection in Text-Attributed Graphs (LECT), which integrates large language models (LLMs) and energy-based contrastive learning. The proposed method involves generating high-quality OOD samples by leveraging the semantic understanding and contextual knowledge of LLMs to create dependency-aware pseudo-OOD nodes, and applying contrastive learning based on energy functions to distinguish between in-distribution (IND) and OOD nodes. The effectiveness of our method is demonstrated through extensive experiments on six benchmark datasets, where our method consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, achieving both high classification accuracy and robust OOD detection capabilities.
2603.20293.paper_summary_zh: 文本屬性圖，其中節點被豐富了文本屬性，已成為建模現實世界網絡（如引用、社交和交易網絡）的強大工具。<lb/>然而，現有的從這些圖中學習的方法通常假設訓練和測試數據的分佈是一致的。<lb/>這一假設在面對分佈外（OOD）數據時會導致顯著的性能下降。<lb/>在本文中，我們解決了文本屬性圖中節點級OOD檢測的挑戰，旨在在準確的節點分類的同時識別OOD節點。<lb/>我們提出了一種新穎的方法，即用於文本屬性圖的分佈外檢測的LLM增強能量對比學習（LECT），該方法整合了大型語言模型（LLMs）和基於能量的對比學習。<lb/>所提出的方法涉及通過利用LLMs的語義理解和上下文知識生成高質量的OOD樣本，以創建依賴感知的偽OOD節點，並應用基於能量函數的對比學習來區分分佈內（IND）和OOD節點。<lb/>我們的方法的有效性通過在六個基準數據集上的廣泛實驗得到了證明，我們的方法始終超越最先進的基準，實現了高分類準確性和穩健的OOD檢測能力。
2603.20293.author: Xiaoxu Ma et.al.
2603.20293.authors: Xiaoxu Ma, Dong Li, Minglai Shao, Xintao Wu, Chen Zhao
